message:
chore(beads): file 5 security-audit gap beads (cc_1)

Applied /security-audit-for-saas to crates/frankenterm-core/.
frankenterm isn't a SaaS but has analogous concerns — agents are
tenants, the policy engine is RBAC, the redactor is the trust
boundary. The five beads cover concrete exploitable defects, each
with a reproduction sketch and an attack scenario the maintainers
would recognize.

ft-3xek9  Redactor blocklist — newer AI provider tokens (xAI,
          Groq, Cohere, raw JWT in JSON without bearer-keyword)
          leak unredacted through robot/MCP/audit despite the
          README's "redact secret material in returned text"
          claim. Concrete fix: 5 prefix patterns + raw-JWT
          fallback + provider-matrix regression gate.

ft-nrqf7  session_topology::from_json — no recursion limit, no
          size limit. A crafted .ft/snapshots/ file blows the
          stack (recursion bomb at depth 200) or OOMs (1GB
          extra HashMap). Per skill Axiom 7 (recovery path is
          shadow codebase), restore is THE highest-stakes
          security surface. Existing fuzz target is bounded
          and doesn't catch this.

ft-j0ufc  Workflow runner privilege amplification — pattern
          detection on attacker-controlled pane output drives
          policy-gated actions on OTHER panes. Per skill ⊙
          Identity-Chain Trace operator: the source-pane trust
          relationship to action-pane is unverified. Fix:
          source-pane allowlist on Workflow trait + audit-log
          (source, target) edges.

ft-3se13  Policy decision log captures attacker text via
          PolicyInput.command_text without redaction. The
          redactor IS wired to AuditActionRecord storage path
          (storage.rs:2966) but NOT to the policy decision log
          (policy.rs:4906). Result: years of operator typing
          accumulates plaintext secrets in the audit table.
          Reproduction shown via direct sqlite query.

ft-0ctwe  ft robot send <pane> "<text>" — secret-bearing text
          passes through argv. Visible to every process under
          same UID via ps, /proc/*/cmdline, auditd, NTM's send-
          key history, shell history. The redactor cannot help
          here (text is captured before any ft code runs). Fix:
          --text-from-stdin / --text-from-file flags + argv
          warning when text matches a redactor pattern + README
          guidance.

Each finding includes file:line references, an attack scenario
mapped to a skill operator card or axiom, and a concrete fix.

Excluded as not-a-finding after investigation:
  - FTS5 query injection (parameterized; ?-bound; safe)
  - authorize_preview rate-limit bypass (preview path returns
    decision without performing action; this is correct dry-run
    behavior)
  - MCP get_text response unredacted (verified: engine.redact_secrets
    IS applied at mcp_tools.rs:1624)
  - agent-mail token storage (handled by external mcp_agent_mail
    binary; not in core's surface)

Audit ran in <30 minutes. Stopped at 5 findings per cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
